perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be construed and interpreted in accordance with the laws of the State of Idaho. The parties agree that the courts of Idaho shall have exclusive jurisdiction and agree that Ada County is the proper venue.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be construed and interpreted in accordance with the laws of the State of Idaho.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause specifying exclusive jurisdiction in Idaho courts and venue in Ada County, the agreement becomes vague on where disputes must be resolved. This can lead to forum shopping and increased litigation costs if a dispute arises, potentially conflicting with Idaho law regarding contractual obligations.
perturbation.law_explanation: Idaho Code § 29-110 addresses the validity of forum selection clauses. While it allows for such clauses, removing an explicit clause makes the agreement ambiguous, which may make it harder to enforce the contract's intended venue, and could open the door to litigation in other jurisdictions, raising questions about whether the parties truly agreed on Idaho jurisdiction.

Universal Citation: ID Code § 29-110 (2022) Learn more This media-neutral citation is based on the American Association of Law Libraries Universal Citation Guide and is not necessarily the official citation. Previous Next 29-110. LIMITATIONS ON RIGHT TO SUE UNDER CONTRACT OR FRANCHISE AGREEMENT. (1) Every stipulation or condition in a contract, by which any party thereto is restricted from enforcing his rights under the contract in Idaho tribunals, or which limits the time within which he may thus enforce his rights, is void as it is against the public policy of Idaho. Nothing in this section shall affect contract provisions relating to arbitration so long as the contract does not require arbitration to be conducted outside the state of Idaho. (2) Any condition, stipulation or provision in a franchise agreement is void to the extent it purports to waive, or has the effect of waiving, venue or jurisdiction of the state of Idaho’s court system. Any condition, stipulation or provision in a franchise agreement, to the extent it purports to assert, or has the effect of asserting, the choice of law is enforceable. This subsection shall apply to any franchise agreement entered into or renewed on or after July 1, 2003, by any person who at the time of entering into or renewing such franchise agreement was a resident of this state or incorporated or organized under the laws of this state. (3) As used in this section "franchise agreement" means a written contract or agreement by which: (a) A person ("franchise
perturbation.justification: The original contract specified exclusive jurisdiction in Idaho courts and venue in Ada County. The changed text removes this, which creates a structural flaw by making the venue for dispute resolution ambiguous. Idaho Code § 29-110(2) states that any provision in a franchise agreement is void to the extent it purports to waive venue or jurisdiction of the state of Idaho’s court system. While the snippet relates to franchise agreements, the removal of the venue clause introduces uncertainty. This contradicts the legal intent by potentially allowing disputes to be resolved outside of Idaho, increasing litigation costs and conflicting with Idaho law, which the original clause sought to avoid. The law snippet supports that restrictions from enforcing rights under the contract in Idaho tribunals are against public policy.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding the preceding, the Company understands and agrees as follows: (a) MARIPOSA shall have the right to disclose Confidential Information to the extent required by applicable law or regulation
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding the preceding, the Company understands and agrees as follows: (a) MARIPOSA shall have the right to disclose Confidential Information to the extent required by applicable law or regulation
perturbation.explanation: Moving this provision, which allows disclosure when required by law, to a section labeled 'Optional Understandings' undermines its mandatory nature. It suggests that compliance with laws regarding disclosure of confidential information is optional, which is incorrect and potentially illegal.
perturbation.law_explanation: The Idaho Trade Secrets Act § 48-803 provides exceptions for disclosure required by law. Presenting this provision as an 'optional understanding' casts doubt on whether the company genuinely acknowledges and accepts the legally mandated exceptions for disclosing trade secrets, and increases the likelihood of challenges against mandatory disclosures.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: Idaho Code Section 48-803 (2022) - DAMAGES. :: 2022 Idaho Code and Statutes :: U.S. Codes and Statutes :: U.S. Law :: Justia There Is a Newer Version of this Section 2024 Idaho Code 2023 Idaho Code 2022 Idaho Code (here) View All Versions View Our Newest Version Here 2022 Idaho Code Title 48 - MONOPOLIES AND TRADE PRACTICES Chapter 8 - IDAHO TRADE SECRETS ACT Section 48-803 - DAMAGES. Universal Citation: ID Code § 48-803 (2022) Learn more This media-neutral citation is based on the American Association of Law Libraries Universal Citation Guide and is not necessarily the official citation. Previous Next 48-803. DAMAGES. (1) Except to the extent that a material and prejudicial change of position prior to acquiring knowledge or reason to know of misappropriation renders a monetary recovery inequitable, a complainant is entitled to recover damages for misappropriation. Damages can include both the actual loss caused by misappropriation and the unjust enrichment caused by misappropriation that is not taken into account in computing actual loss. In lieu of damages measured by any other methods, the damages caused by misappropriation may be measured by imposition of liability for a reasonable royalty for a misappropriator’s unauthorized disclosure or use of a trade secret. (2) If willful and malicious misappropriation exists, the court may award exemplary damages in an amount not exceeding twice any award made under subsection (1) of this section. perturbation.justification: The perturbation involves a structural flaw where a provision allowing disclosure of confidential information when required by law is moved to a section labeled 'Optional Understandings.' This undermines the mandatory nature of compliance with laws regarding disclosure, as it suggests that compliance is optional. Idaho Code § 48-803 discusses damages for misappropriation of trade secrets. By presenting the disclosure exception as optional, the modified contract casts doubt on the company's genuine acknowledgment and acceptance of legally mandated exceptions for disclosing trade secrets. This creates a legal contradiction because the original text acknowledges that MARIPOSA shall have the right to disclose confidential information to the extent required by law, but this mandatory acknowledgment is undermined by framing it as an 'optional understanding' in the changed text. This contradicts the principle that certain disclosures are legally mandated and not optional.
